name: The Pirate Queen with Lucy Liu

reviews.review_description: Storyline and figuring stuff out is good. I enjoy climbing the ropes. Putting out fires during the cannon battle really immersed me as I frantically ran around. I picked this game up for a very low price and this is likely because of how glitchy it is. When you pick up an item, it often does not put it in the inventory yet if you quit and resume later, it will be there. I am now stuck where I raise the red flag. I have raised it, it shows it flapping in the wind, but the game will not progress because the flag still shows in my inventory, no matter how many times I pull it out and “re-raise” the flag. I have quit and powered off and come back a different day, and nothing fixes this. I guess this is where the game ends for me. I would have liked to see how it was supposed to end.

reviews.review_title: The best man for the job, is a WOMAN.
reviews.review_description:
This is a fun little playthrough game that can be finished in an hour and a half to two hours depending on how good you are at solving problems, puzzles and exploring.  
You play the wife of a famous and powerful Pirate.  You share in the operations of your pirate fleet, but when your husband dies, some of the other pirates decide they don't want a female leader for their group. 
Now you must prove to them that YOU are the one true leader and defeat the enemy to gain the trust of all others. 
This is a story driven game that is more like a short story that you get to act out one of the parts.  You can go at your own pace, enjoy the story and scenery or power through to get to the last page of the story. 
I did not have any glitch or any other problems mentioned in a few other reviews.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the thoughtful review! ⭐

We’re really glad you enjoyed the story and Cheng Shih’s journey to leadership. It means a lot to hear that the experience worked well for you  ⚓

Thanks again for playing The Pirate Queen!  ❤️

reviews.review_description: different, fun adventure but glitches are frustrating

reviews.review_title: Not really a game, more of a book
reviews.review_description: This isn't really a game. There's no actual player driven aspect. You just move along to make the story progress, and the movement mechanic is HORRIBLE. Unlike every other game in history (not you Myst) where the left stick walks and the right stick turns, this has some teleport thing on left and a crappy snap-turn thing on right... which will kill your buzz so fast. You end up inevitably moving the left like you're going to walk and Wtf it's a box in my face oh God where am I. And snap turn sucks ALWAYS in any game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback ❤️

Just as a note, there is also a free-roam movement option available in the settings if you prefer that over teleport locomotion and snap turning. 

The Pirate Queen is designed as a story-driven experience, we appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts ⚓

reviews.review_title: I enjoyed it… To an extent
reviews.review_description: Ok. It’s alright. It’s not the worst game I’ve played but it’s not great. Theres bugs everywhere. Voice lines started and stopped every time I did something. And most importantly. The very easy puzzles that I understood in a few minutes. Like take the seasoning puzzle. Why is it color coded?! Descriptions are very good as it gives the player what the different spices are. But sight of colors makes it really easy to solve the puzzle. And puzzles are 80% of this game. I understand you need to let the smaller indie companies go but look at the secret of retrotopulus. Made by the same amount, maybe even less amount of people and I had a better experience with that game. But the good parts of this game was actually pretty fun to go through. I just wished it had more. 3.65/5 stars

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback ❤️

We’re glad you enjoyed parts of the experience. Your notes about puzzle design are really helpful, as we’re continuing to review player comments and improve the game 🔧

Thank you again for playing The Pirate Queen!

reviews.review_description:
I didn't know what to expect from this. At first I thought it would be an adventure game and then I thought it would be a puzzle/escape room game. Turns out that it is a bit of each!

The game can be a bit clunky, but once you get in the swing of it then it starts to really grab you.
The story is interesting! I love the idea of it all, the cultural and historic aspects of it are so intriguing! Cool having Lucy Liu doing the main narration. I like how it all unfolds during one night.
Gameplay wise, I like the climbing segments, which are decent though I swear there were a few times I had a hold but apparently I didn't.  There are some inventive additions to that as well to keep it fresh, like the bells to avoid for alerting enemies, and the rotting pieces of rope that fall apart when you grab them.
Dodging the swinging crates adds some decent platforming elements, and finding the levers give light puzzle solving.

Though I have to say, the first few chapters really set the tone, but I struggled the most with them. Partly because the game can be quite vague with what you need to do at times, and other times because I'm the problem,  just being unobservant and stupid. Like in the first room I'm a collector so I was so concerned with picking up items that might be useful, then I didn't even notice the vases you're supposed to look for. I do have to say that it doesn't make it clear at times what is needed and what isn't. I was so busy thinking that the pipe would need to be used but then found out it wasn't important.
Some items struggle to stand properly back on tables. But it isn't too major of a glitch, just a slight annoying one. Ha. And then in chapter 3 I put the pots in the right places but it didn't register until I moved them around like the ball in the cup and it finally activated.
The collectibles are a good extra to look out for. But sometimes they're easy, other times intuitively placed, then other times ridiculously hard. Similar to the puzzles.
It wasn't until chapter 6 that I managed to do things without having to bother the assistant guy with ringing my bell until I could swear he started to say the same clue with a more annoyed tone at me.

The rowing sections are great cardio! Though there isn't actually any stealth tactics needed, only alluded to. Maybe would have been better if you had to actually time your moves through patrols.

There is a satisfying ending and it wraps up nicely. It's quite short but a mostly satisfying ride. The graphics are nice too. The detail of the flotilla of all the boats is really well done! 

In all i enjoyed this experience and I hope that the developers keep making innovative and culturally positive and intriguing games!
Blabber over.
Great single player story game and has Quest Achievements!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing and for the kind words! ⭐

We’re really glad you enjoyed the story and achievements. It means a lot to our small team ⚓

Thanks for being part of The Pirate Queen community ❤️

reviews.review_description:
The game is very ugly, looks like unfinished. There are very few puzzles and the game has some arcade elements (like climbing or dodging swinging boxes) which extend the playtime, which would otherwise be very short (the game is quite short anyway). Everything makes that game feels like early stage demo.

Two very positive aspects are: the narration by Lucy Liu, and quite clever hint system.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for taking the time to share such thoughtful feedback. We completely understand that the experience won’t be for everyone, as The Pirate Queen is intentionally designed as a focused, narrative-led adventure rather than a long, mechanics-heavy game. We really appreciate you highlighting the narration and hint system, that means a lot to the team.

reviews.review_description: It's a nice narrative adventure puzzle game that is short but worth a playthrough.  Everything mechanically works well and the story is good.  Lucy Lui nailed it and I personally would love to see more experiences like this.  Keep em coming!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words! 🌟

We’re really glad you enjoyed the story and experience, and we’ll be sure to pass your compliments on to Lucy Liu as well! Thanks for playing The Pirate Queen 🏴‍☠️❤️

reviews.review_description: So this game was pretty fun, not too difficult. There were some glitches in the game that to get past I had to close the game out and reopen it a few times. If it had been much longer, it probably would have gotten a little monotonous. Overall, a fun little game.

Walking climbing  linear game. Rather boring.no fighting. Empty world. Just you walking
 around.doing what your told. By the vioce in your head.. gotta few puzzles. Silly really.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts ❤️

The Pirate Queen is designed as a story-driven adventure focused on exploration and narrative. 

We appreciate you giving it a try and taking the time to share your feedback ⚓

reviews.review_description: I love games that put me in a realistic foreign world. This one is another time too! Really interesting and fun  puzzles that aren't frustrating I didn't find!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words ❤️

We’re really glad you enjoyed exploring the world and solving the puzzles. Thanks for playing The Pirate Queen! 🌟

reviews.review_description:
It is very nice, easy puzzles, beautiful story, very different to play as a female Asian pirate! However I can't continue to play as they put gods and religious stuff in the game. (personal preference)
I did have some difficulties with movement etc when playing and reaching stuff, I play seated and had to keep using the settings to adjust height. You also walk really slow, would have been good to have a sprint option.

 The hints aren't great at all. Why say one drop of poison will do, but keep telling me there is something else to find in the room? When in fact it was nothing to find I just needed to empty the whole bottle of poison.
Mostly great escape rooms with a story. Great voice acting.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words ⭐
We’re really glad you enjoyed the puzzles and voice acting. Thanks for playing The Pirate Queen 🏴‍☠️❤️

reviews.review_description:
The game is super short. Think like 2 hours. You
 do not really need to think as everything is super easy. Most of the time you will be in a small boat that only goes on a "rail"

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback! ❤️

The Pirate Queen is designed as a story-driven adventure that can be experienced in a single sitting. 

We so appreciate you giving it a try and sharing your thoughts ⚓

reviews.review_description: Loved the storyline, wish there were more like this

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words 🌟

We’re really glad you enjoyed the story. We hope you’ll join us again if the adventure continues 🏴‍☠️

Reviews like this mean so much to us - thank you for your support ❤️

reviews.review_description:
This is one of my top 5 favorite games on Oculus. It wasn't too hard or too easy to navigate the puzzles. I got stuck a couple of times but I love that you can get hints a little or more at a time, if you want. 
The graphics was really beautiful at times. Super nice game!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing review ❤️

We’re absolutely thrilled to hear The Pirate Queen is one of your top 5 games! It means a lot that you enjoyed the puzzles, hint system, and the world we created. 🌟

Thanks so much for playing and for the support 💖🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_description: It is fairly OK for what it is.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving The Pirate Queen a try 😊

We appreciate you taking the time to leave a review.

reviews.review_description:
Love the game, a bit too short.
Finished it in a couple hours.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for playing and for the kind words ❤️
We’re really glad you enjoyed the experience, and we appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts 🌊

reviews.review_description: It's a beautiful game with great visuals and some nice puzzles. It's fairly short and easy to get through. Definitely worth buying it and going through it!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words 🌟 It really means a lot to us! 

We’re so glad you enjoyed the visuals and puzzles. Thanks for playing The Pirate Queen and sharing your experience 🏴‍☠️

reviews.review_description: Though simple, the immersive game was immersive and fun! Some of the puzzles/game interactions were not obvious but that's real life. But hearing Lucy Liu narrate the game was the biggest plus! Love live Queen Liu!

developer_response.body: ❤️ 🏴‍☠️👑 Thanks so much for playing!!

reviews.review_description: It’s a short game, but quite fun. Story was decent and visuals were nice. I recommend as long as you are ok with the short run time (about 2 hours)

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for delving into The Pirate Queen and taking the time to review, we're delighted you enjoyed the experience and shared your kind words!

reviews.review_description: During the tutorial you need to kayak to your ship and I got super dizzy. Then you have to climb a rope ladder and that hit pretty hard too. It was fine after that but once you get all dizzy you don't want to play any VR anymore. If you're sensitive to backgrounds moving you may feel icky at these parts. Not sure about the rest of the game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your experience ❤️

We’re sorry to hear the opening moments caused discomfort. Motion sensitivity can vary between players, the teleport locomotion option in the settings may help reduce this and many players find it more comfortable 🌟

We appreciate you giving The Pirate Queen a try.

reviews.review_description:
The graphics and the story are stunning.
It's a short 1.5 hour adventure with lovely narration and practical ways to keep the game low-budget without sacrificing the immersion.  The puzzles were just way too easy.  It felt more like a story than a game...  No compliants, I'll replay it to find all of the collectables :-p

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing the game and taking the time to review, we're so pleased you enjoyed the story and proud that you managed to smash all the puzzles so easily! Well done Queen! ✨👑

reviews.review_description:
I usually don't write bad reviews but I have to say I was really disappointed with this game. I love the story of the pirate Queen. She was such an interesting person. And I love Lucy Liu (amazing actress!). So when this game was announced I was super excited. 

However, it is painfully boring. The story barely scratches the real story, and even worse the VR gameplay would have been basic back in 2016. It is essentially just climbing, grabbing and turning things. Really wish they had done something interesting with this amazing story.

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you didn't enjoy the experience HarbingerThrace. We're really proud to have created an non-combat story-led experience that shines a light on an incredible female leader from history.  Thanks for taking the time to check it out - we're excited for everyone to learn about her incredible story too!

reviews.review_description: Great story driven adventure ,this game is about entertainment. In a heavy story driven way , it's fabulous, graphics gorgeous with puzzle elements.  Highly recommend and don't listen to blind one below me he needs glasses as he can't  see how fantastic the graphic are, he's a paid competitor more than likely

developer_response.body: Thanks Bozz Greg for your swashbuckling review! It's great to hear which parts of The Pirate Queen you enjoyed the most  - happy pirating.

reviews.review_title: An epic step through time into adventure
reviews.review_description:
Pirate Queen is hands down the best action adventure game available on Quest, or arguably on any headset. Making games adaptable to VR is tricky, and here we have a game singer studios have geared towards realistic, first person physics from the start. 

Narration and voice acting by none other than Lucy Liu - who better to accompany us on a swashbuckling adventure through choppy waters on the eve of a coup. In the dead of the night you find yourself ready to take on a pirate rebellion. This is only the beginning, I can't wait for more installments of the Pirate Queen!

developer_response.body: So glad you enjoyed the experience Stasia.nico!

reviews.review_description:
Thought would buy and see what it is like,only got to chapter 4 but so far no bugs and the graphics look quite good. Story is slowly  moving along but enjoyable.  Only problem had was grabbing scroll in bottom drawer but once lowered my height in options got it first time…love the voice ,think it is Lucy Liu

P.s. no one’s paying me to give a good review !!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback and for flagging the bug - we've now updated the game so this should be working now. Happy pirating!

reviews.review_description: I absolutely enjoyed this and it ended up being more than I expected! This is advertised as an interactive story which I thoroughly enjoy already, but to my pleasant surprise there was an escape room mode to choose. Very professionally done, great voice acting with Lucy Liu, with the perfect balance of puzzle escape room tasks, climbing, and rowing. My only one true complaint is that I wanted more. I didn't want it to be over and although I knew it would be short, this could benefit from having some added content to the story. All in all I was pleasantly surprised and I would highly recommend

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the feedback! So glad you enjoyed the experience of The Pirate Queen

reviews.review_description:
Quite a good game, nice graphics, nice music, fairly easy puzzles but it’s way, way too short and you finish it far too quickly and feel a bit let down at the end. In fact I felt I was just getting into the game and it finished.

The puzzles are pretty straight forward, some are just trial and error, like the scales which is a bit poor and perhaps lazy.

Finding the collectibles does add some time to the game and another dimension but there is no back story with them, it’s like they are there just to extend the game and that is a missed opportunity I think.

Is it worth the money, to be honest I think it should be cheaper really when you compare it to some other games in the same price bracket.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback!

reviews.review_description:
This game is getting the "female protagonist" review hit, and I knew it would be artificially low. If you hate women, don't buy this game, obviously you won't like it, it's based on the true story of an actual woman who did the things you pretend to do in VR.

Playing on Quest 3, just finished the first chapter and paused to review because this game is beautiful. and I knew male reviewers would be bombing it.  I've encountered no issues, all of the mechanics worked. I am absolutely hooked on how the tale unfolds; exceptional acting and direction. A little more game than interactive story, as far as I can tell so far, it offers two modes: more hints/help and what they call "Escape Room."
If I encounter issues, I'll update, but so far I really love it and don't want to play it too quickly.

Such an immersive feeling of time and place. Looking forward to playing more! Don't buy it if stories about women make you want to cry into your basement Mountain Dew. This is why I don't do strangers' friend requests.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback, we really appreciate you taking the time to leave us your thoughts! We're so happy you enjoyed the game, happy pirating!

reviews.review_description:
Anybody writing "no bugs" or "gorgeous graphics!" is either tripping or paid to write it. As far as a technical rating goes, this gets 1 star. Buggy and lousy graphics on Quest 3, with pop-ins close to center view at like 5ft away.

Why I still give 4 stars: Even with the Playstation 1/2 era graphics and beta-release type bugs, it still manages to pull you out of your world and into hers. 3 stars. Pulling that off for under $20: 4 stars. 

Give this experience a graphical overhaul, (I'm pretty sure the Quest 2 could handle double the textures), fix the bugs, and it's easy a 5 star deal.. Hell, charge $5 more for a Quest 3 graphics and lighting package and it'd be 5 stars and worth it.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your feedback, we really appreciate you taking the time to review the game. We have updated the build and fixed a number of bugs, resovled the LOD pop-ins and fixed the locomotion issues players have been facing. We're driven to make the best version of The Pirate Queen possible and are really grateful to you taking the time to share your thoughts. If we can be of any more help please feel free to reach out to us - we hope you enjoy pirating on the South China Sea!

reviews.review_description: I am only on chapter 4 and I'm stuck at a puzzle that won't allow me to continue.  I am doing it correctly.  I watched YouTube videos to confirm it.  It just won't let me continue.  Real annoying.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback - we've updated the build to resolve the bug in chapter 4. We really appreciate you taking the time to flag this and we hope you continue to enjoy the game - we're here to help if you have any further issues.

reviews.review_description: No,even one npc anf story line is hirribly short

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for taking the time to experience the game! We've updated the build and added in achievements to extend gameplay, so in the spirit of Piracy - we challenge you to find all 33 of the collectables and see how well you fare as a true Pirate Queen - we'd love to hear how you get on!

reviews.review_title: An Experience more than a Game
reviews.review_description: Full Review on YouTube: @superhippieman

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to feedback! We appreciate you taking the time to enjoy The Pirate Queen!

reviews.review_description:
I was really pleasantly surprised by this indie game - as a bit of a history nerd, finding a game that  faithfully recreates the past is always a delight, because so many get it so wrong or fall into stereotype, etc. Stepping into 19th century China felt like an immersive journey through time. The attention to detail is remarkable. The first thing you see is a vast, expansive sky and then the imposing presence of Cheng Shih's pirate ship, which felt awesome. Playing a female character (especially one inspired by the legendary Cheng Shih) was awesome too - when playing this I realised that I rarely look down at my hands in VR and see that they belong to a woman! 

For me though what really sets this game apart are the meticulous attention to details throughout. It's not just about the grand spectacle, but the subtle intricacies like the detailed objects, the typography, the collectibles (couldn't find them all, going to try again with a separate playthrough), and even the language throughout. I felt invited to delve deeper into the narrative to unravel the mysteries of Cheng Shih's world.

This isn't your typical combat-centric RPG; rather, it's an immersive exploration into a beautifully rendered environment, a journey of discovery, puzzle-solving, and uncovering a story inspired by real historical events. If you'll pardon the pun, it's a treasure trove if you're a puzzle-seeker or history-enthusiast, or if you simply love a good story told wonderfully by none other than Lucy Liu.

I've heard that The Pirate Queen is going to be expanded into a graphic novel and film - which is a testament to the boundless creativity of the indie studio behind this great wee game. While some may find the game relatively short compared to big studio RPGs like Assassin's Creed, I found it to be a refreshing take on the pirate genre. Thoroughly recommend!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your detailed feedback Lollipopluc - we appreciate you!

reviews.review_description: I just finished The Pirate Queen. I really enjoyed the story. It really pulls you in the further you progress. You must overcome obstacles and puzzles to achieve your goal of becoming the Pirate Queen. I thought the Sea, ships, and dark environment created a mood that fit the game well. The length of the game is not that long since I beat it in around 4 hours. It's still a fun experience I enjoyed throughout. The vocals for the narration throughout the game is perfect. Very clear and keeps you informed of what's going on. My only complaint is I wish there was some type of character interaction. You come across characters throughout the game but it's more of like a shadowy type figure. I just feel some better character interactions would have added better visualization to the plot at hand. Still, a solid fun game that is priced very reasonably. Happy I made the purchase.

developer_response.body: Thank for the feedback edward33.421681! So glad you enjoyed the experience of The Pirate Queen

reviews.review_description: I had high expectations after games like Red Matter and The Room. This game is nowhere near those in gameplay.

developer_response.body: Hi S00p3rMom, thanks so much for taking the time to share your thoughts! The Pirate Queen is an interactive story so we appreciate the experience is not as challenging as puzzle games like Red Matter or The Room, instead its a story-rich experience where you step back in time and learn about an incredible leader from history! Would you mind giving us a bit more detail on what you found glitchy? We'd love to fix this for you. Here's our email: hello@singerstudios.co.uk

reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed playing the game. One only glitch while fishing. Some puzzles were quite hard but good story and graphics.

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed The Pirate Queen! Thank you for your feedback

reviews.review_title: Good But Not Worth Current Price
reviews.review_description:
The Pirate Queen is a narrative, immersive, escape room-style experience, that takes you on an adventure during the night Cheng Shih came to power. Through this brief, extremely short journey you will encounter climbing ropes, which is kinda fun. Solving puzzles, which are designed for toddlers. Navigating gauntlet-style obstacles that a blind person could do with their hands tied behind their backs. And I haven't even begun to mention the walking through walls and getting stuck in them, and the whole popup window and black screens when your head gets too close to supposed objects that are in essence, ethereal. The whole supposed experience was over in under 2 hours and left me feeling quite disappointed. The story was great but way too short, the delightful Lucy Liu was great, as always, and the graphics were reasonable, and that's being modest. The game just doesn't make sense to buy at its current price, for a subpar experience, that has zero replayability. I think the right price point for this game should be more around 10 US Dollars, and my review rating would be higher. And they should consider adding additional harder, more challenging settings. You just really don't get what you're paying for.

I rate this game 3.5 stars out of 5. But I have rated it a 4/5 here due to not being able to do increments, and the game doesn't deserve a 3/5.

If the immersion breaking popup that keeps coming up every time my head bangs into an object is removed, and the whole walking through and putting your hands through nearly every single solid object is fixed, my rating would go from a 3.5 to a solid 4. And then if the gameplay was longer, or new harder challenges were put in place for a 3rd difficulty mode, my rating would go up to 4.5 stars.

developer_response.body: Hey Slayerazazel, thanks so much for taking the time to feedback! We've updated the build to reduce the black popups and updated textures to improve graphics. We appreciate the candid feedback, so thanks for playing! We're delighted you enjoyed listening to Lucy's performance!
